questions.question: What is the primary function of a stack data structure?
options: A) To store data in a sorted order
B) To retrieve data in the order it was added
C) To add and remove elements from the top
D) To store and retrieve data randomly
questions.explanation: A stack is a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) data structure, meaning the last element added will be the first one to be removed.

questions.question: Which data structure is used to store and retrieve elements in a random order?
questions.explanation: A hash table is a data structure that stores key-value pairs and allows for efficient retrieval of elements using their keys.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between a queue and a stack?
options: A) A queue follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle
B) A queue allows elements to be added and removed from the end
C) A queue allows elements to be added and removed from the top
D) A queue is used for sorting data
questions.explanation: A queue follows the FIFO principle, meaning the first element added will be the first one to be removed.

questions.question: Which data structure is used to store a collection of elements in a specific order?
questions.explanation: An array is a data structure that stores elements in a specific order and allows for efficient retrieval of elements using their indices.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of searching for an element in a hash table?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of searching for an element in a hash table is O(1), making it one of the most efficient data structures for this operation.
